content:
Hi there,

There's an unmerged PR on github with debugfs that uses OpenAdapter. However rust/kernel/file.rs was removed somewhere in v6.1, instead rust/kernel/fs/file.rs was introduced without any OpenAdapter functionality. Are there any pending patches or reasons why it was removed?

Code gets upstreamed to the Linux kernel as needed, since we cannot push unused code. v6.1 was the time Rust got merged into the kernel, but not everything was submitted. So it is rather that it got never added, rather than that it was removed.

Please see e.g. https://rust-for-linux.com/branches#rust and https://rust-for-linux.com/contributing#submitting-new-abstractions-and-modules for more information.

I hope that clarifies.

Just fyi, in case debugfs is from interest here: @Fabien Parent has 3 patches in his rust-debugfs branch (the 3 top ones). Yes, they not entirely up to date, either. But using fs/file.rs instead, KVec instead of Vec and some other minor adaptions they should be portable to recent kernel relatively easily. In case you like to do that please let us know any issues you find doing that :grinning:

Yup, thanks to their commits I was able to port debugfs to v6.14-rc6: https://github.com/msm8227-mainline/linux/commits/msm8227-6.14-rust (please don't mind the commit messages, it's a playground).

The issue is the read function, which is called every time the buffer is larger than 4kb. seq_file is a possible solution, and there's pr, but it uses old debugfs implementation with Box<Box<dyn Any>>, while the current one is Pin<KBox<Registration>>. I'm not that deep into FFI and haven't found examples of how to do bindings with Rust <-> C callbacks correctly
